top_signals.title: China’s Oil Imports May Be Set to Recover as Stockpiling Returns
top_signals.summary: China’s crude imports look poised to recover from a months-long slump as the country relaxes fuel export curbs, raises run rates and snaps up prompt Middle East supplies, with analysts and traders forecasting a return to strategic stockpiling later this year.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: South Africa Plans First Strategic Oil Boost Since Apartheid Era
top_signals.summary: South Africa plans to increase its strategic oil reserves for the first time since the apartheid government stockpiled crude, adding to measures across the continent to mitigate supply shocks.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: License plate cameras may be next target after Supreme Court reins in location tracking
top_signals.summary: If a warrant is ultimately needed for ALPR searches, experts say, it would radically limit how the networks of cameras can be used and would change modern policing.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.
